From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

1921 British silent film by Thomas Bentley

| The Adventures of Mr. Pickwick | |
| --- | --- |
| Directed by | [Thomas Bentley](/wiki/Thomas_Bentley_(director) "Thomas Bentley (director)") |
| Written by | [Charles Dickens](/wiki/Charles_Dickens "Charles Dickens") (novel) G. A. Baughan [Eliot Stannard](/wiki/Eliot_Stannard "Eliot Stannard") |
| Starring | [Frederick Volpe](/wiki/Frederick_Volpe "Frederick Volpe") [Mary Brough](/wiki/Mary_Brough "Mary Brough") [Bransby Williams](/wiki/Bransby_Williams "Bransby Williams") [Ernest Thesiger](/wiki/Ernest_Thesiger "Ernest Thesiger") |
| Production company | [Ideal Film Company](/wiki/Ideal_Film_Company "Ideal Film Company") |
| Distributed by | Ideal Film Company |
| Release date | * November 1921 (1921-11) |
| Country | United Kingdom |
| Language | Silent |

***The Adventures of Mr. Pickwick*** is a 1921 British [silent](/wiki/Silent_film "Silent film") [comedy film](/wiki/Comedy_film "Comedy film") directed by [Thomas Bentley](/wiki/Thomas_Bentley_(director) "Thomas Bentley (director)") based on the 1837 novel *[The Pickwick Papers](/wiki/The_Pickwick_Papers "The Pickwick Papers")* by [Charles Dickens](/wiki/Charles_Dickens "Charles Dickens").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The film is missing from the [BFI National Archive](/wiki/BFI_National_Archive "BFI National Archive"), and is listed as one of the [British Film Institute](/wiki/British_Film_Institute "British Film Institute")'s "[75 Most Wanted](/wiki/BFI_75_Most_Wanted "BFI 75 Most Wanted")" lost films.[[2]](#cite_note-BFI-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Adventures_of_Mr._Pickwick&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

A contemporary review in [*Kinematograph Weekly*](/wiki/Kinematograph_Weekly "Kinematograph Weekly") outlined the plot as follows:[[4]](#cite_note-4)

> Opens with the forming of the Pickwick Club and introductions to its members, and is followed by Mr. Pickwick's instructions to his landlady, Mrs. Bardell, being misinterpreted by her as a proposal. The party travel by coach to Rochester, where the episode of Mr. Pickwick's entry into the wrong bedroom occurs. Mrs. Bardell visits a lawyer with a view to taking proceeding against her lodge. The party are seen at Dingley Dell. Mr. Tupman declares his intentions to Miss Rachael, who is persuaded to elope with Alfred Jingle. Mr. Winkle experiments with a gun; the whole party plays cricket. The eloping couple are stopped by Mr. Pickwick, who returns home to find his case has come into Court. The trial takes place, and the whole is concluded by a dance at Dingley Dell, in which Mr. Pickwick joins.

## Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Adventures_of_Mr._Pickwick&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast")]

* [Frederick Volpe](/wiki/Frederick_Volpe "Frederick Volpe") as [Samuel Pickwick](/wiki/Samuel_Pickwick "Samuel Pickwick") (as Fred Volpe)
* [Mary Brough](/wiki/Mary_Brough "Mary Brough") as [Mrs Bardell](/wiki/Mrs_Bardell_(Pickwick_Papers) "Mrs Bardell (Pickwick Papers)")
* [Bransby Williams](/wiki/Bransby_Williams "Bransby Williams") as Sgt Buzfuz
* [Ernest Thesiger](/wiki/Ernest_Thesiger "Ernest Thesiger") as Mr Jingle
* [Kathleen Vaughan](/wiki/Kathleen_Vaughan "Kathleen Vaughan") as Arabella Allen
* Joyce Dearsley as Isabella Wardle
* [Arthur Cleave](/wiki/Arthur_Cleave "Arthur Cleave") as Mr [Nathaniel Winkle](/wiki/Nathaniel_Winkle "Nathaniel Winkle")
* [Athene Seyler](/wiki/Athene_Seyler "Athene Seyler") as Rachel Wardle
* John Kelt as Mr. [Augustus Snodgrass](/wiki/Augustus_Snodgrass "Augustus Snodgrass")
* Hubert Woodward as [Sam Weller](/wiki/Sam_Weller_(character) "Sam Weller (character)")
* [Norman Page](/wiki/Norman_Page "Norman Page") as Justice Stoneleigh

## Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Adventures_of_Mr._Pickwick&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Reception")]

*Kinematograph Weekly* considered the film as being "probably an exceptionally good booking proposition", praising it for being exceptionally well made by Bentley and highlighted the performance of Volpe as Pickwick as remarkable amongst, before concluding that "Dickens lovers have a treat in store."[[5]](#cite_note-5)

[*The Daily News*](/wiki/The_Daily_News_(UK) "The Daily News (UK)") said that "the true spirit of Dickens, in his most genial and human vein has been caught more completely than in any stage play."[[6]](#cite_note-6)
